duplicateAudienceModal.title: Duplicate audience
duplicateAudienceModal.description:
Any targeting criteria, screener questions, and confidentiality terms that you've set will be copied over. You can edit these later.

Your new audience will be added to the "Audiences" section.

deleteAudienceModal.description: This audience will be permanently deleted and removed from "Audiences" section.

answerQuota.ineligibleTooltipMustSelectAll: Quotas aren't available when all "Must select" answers must be selected.

screeners.availableScreeners: {{count}} screener questions per audience included in your subscription
screeners.addLogicTooltip: Logic skips participants forward. Include another question to add logic.

screenerSuggestion.checkScreenerTooltip: Check for common screener mistakes. This feature is currently optimized for screeners written in English.
screenerSuggestion.checkScreenerIncompleteQuestionsText: Complete all questions before checking your screener
screenerSuggestion.checkScreenerError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

NO_ESCAPE.title: Suggestion: Consider adding an answer for when none of the options apply
NO_ESCAPE.description: Add an option such as "none of the above" or "other" so participants won't select an option that's not applicable to them. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
NO_ESCAPE.summary: Consider adding an answer for when none of the options apply

LEADING.title: Suggestion: Consider rephrasing to avoid leading people to certain answers
LEADING.description: People might feel inclined to give you certain answers if they can only choose between "yes", "no", or fewer than 3 answers. Rephrase the question to get the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
LEADING.summary: Consider rephrasing to avoid leading people to certain answers

LEADING_BINARY.title: Suggestion: Consider rephrasing to avoid leading people to certain answers
LEADING_BINARY.description: People might feel inclined to give you certain answers if they can only choose between "yes", "no", or fewer than 3 answers. Rephrase the question to get the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
LEADING_BINARY.summary: Consider rephrasing to avoid leading people to certain answers

LEADING_NOT_ENOUGH_ANSWERS.title: Suggestion: Consider adding more answers to avoid leading people to certain answers
LEADING_NOT_ENOUGH_ANSWERS.description: People might feel inclined to give you certain answers if they can only choose between fewer than 2 or 3 answers. Add more answers to get the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
LEADING_NOT_ENOUGH_ANSWERS.summary: Consider adding more answers to avoid leading people to certain answers

VAGUE_FREQUENCY.title: Suggestion: Consider providing answers with specific frequencies
VAGUE_FREQUENCY.description: When asking people to describe frequency, provide options that are precise to get accurate answers and the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
VAGUE_FREQUENCY.summary: Consider providing answers with specific frequencies

VAGUE_QUANTITY.title: Suggestion: Consider providing answers with specific quantities
VAGUE_QUANTITY.description: Your answers might be too vague. Define quantity options precisely to get accurate answers and the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
VAGUE_QUANTITY.summary: Consider providing answers with specific quantities

VAGUE_SCALE.title: Suggestion: Consider using a well-defined rating scale to get specific answers
VAGUE_SCALE.description: When asking for someone's level of agreement, experience or familiarity with something, provide a numeric rating scale to get accurate answers and the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
VAGUE_SCALE.summary: Consider using a well-defined rating scale to get specific answers

OVERLAPPING.title: Suggestion: Provide a range of values that are comprehensive and don't overlap
OVERLAPPING.description: When asking for numerical values, provide specific and mutually exclusive options to get accurate answers and the participants you want. <bestPracticesLink>Learn more about screener best practices</bestPracticesLink>
OVERLAPPING.summary: Provide a range of values that are comprehensive and don't overlap

body.inviteLinkToPanel:
The recruitment method you’re changing to **does not support:**
  • An unlimited number of participants
  • The ability to request their email address
The audience name, number of participants (if not unlimited), and any screener questions added will switch to your new recruitment method.
body.panelToInviteLink:
If you change the recruitment method **any target criteria** set for this audience will be **deleted**.

The audience name, number of participants, and any screener questions added will switch to your new recruitment method.

cardSection.title: Customizable text
cardSection.contentText: In this test we will collect your feedback and information for a research project. This information will be encrypted and protected in accordance with our security best practices.
cardSection.consentText: I understand that my feedback and information will be collected for a research project
cardSection.consentSubtitle: Only participants who select the checkbox will take the test
cardSection.contentSubtitle: Secure URLs (https only) can be included to create clickable links

legal.noLegal: Ask participants to review and approve any confidentiality terms

infoModal.title: Confidentiality terms
infoModal.body:
We’ve added a default template with generic data privacy text. You can delete it, edit the text, and add secure URLs linking to your specific terms. Links open in a new tab for participants.
Participant data needs to be protected, with region specific laws like the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) now common place.
These laws mean it’s mandatory to ask participants for their explicit consent if you’re collecting any personal information (for example, their name or email).

deleteModal.body: If you're collecting personal information from participants—in some regions, for example the United States or European Union—it’s mandatory to ask for their consent.
deleteModal.title: Delete confidentiality terms?
